Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, with low urine specific gravity and high microalbumin/creatinine ratio, indicating significant renal impairment, likely nephrotic-range proteinuria. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Electrolyte disturbances include elevated chloride. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and further assessment of the etiology.
